act.title: Vijaya Kumaratunga Memorial Hospital Board Act

statutoryBodies.name: Vijaya Kumaratunga Memorial Hospital Board
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Established by Section 2 as a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal. Manages and administers the Vijaya Kumaratunga Memorial Hospital at Seeduwa. 7 members appointed by the Minister: a Chairman (from Foundation nominees), a Ministry representative, a medical practitioner elected by hospital doctors, an eminent professional, and 4 Foundation-nominated representatives. Quorum is 4 members. Board meets at least once per month. Hospital celebrated 25th anniversary in October 2024 with 137 beds.

exOfficio.role: Director General of Health Services (or nominee)
exOfficio.note: Ex-officio member — as provided in Constitution of the Board (S.3)

nominated.note: Selected from persons nominated by the VK Memorial Foundation (S.3)
Nominated by the Secretary to the Ministry (S.3)
Elected by the medical practitioners of the Hospital (S.3)
Appointed by the Minister — a person of recognised eminence (S.3)
Nominated by the VK Memorial Foundation (S.3)

meetings.chairperson: Chairman appointed from Foundation nominees (S.3); if absent, members elect one among themselves to preside (S.5)
meetings.reporting: Audit of accounts under Article 154 of the Constitution (S.13); annual reports tabled in Parliament

powers: Receive grants, subsidies, donations, gifts, and bequests (S.9(a))
Hold, purchase, take on lease, or otherwise acquire movable and immovable property (S.9(b))
Sell, mortgage, lease, exchange, or otherwise dispose of any property vested in the Board (S.9(c))
Appoint, dismiss, and exercise disciplinary control over employees (S.9(d))
Establish and administer schemes for remuneration and personnel management (S.9(e))
Open and maintain bank accounts as approved by the Minister (S.9(f))
Borrow money with the approval of the Minister (S.9(g))
Do all things necessary or expedient for the proper management and administration of the Hospital (S.9(i))
dataGaps: Board meeting minutes and records not publicly accessible
Whether the Board has held regular monthly meetings is not publicly verified
Regulations made under S.14 not publicly compiled or accessible
Current financial status of the Fund of the Board (S.12) not publicly documented
Relationship with the VK Memorial Foundation's Board of Management not formally documented
Whether Consultative Committees have been established under S.14 is unknown

statutoryBodies.name: Committee of Management
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Established by Section 10 for the day-to-day administration and management of the Hospital. Chaired by the Director of the Hospital. Includes senior clinical and administrative staff plus a representative of the VK Memorial Foundation. The Board Chairman may attend meetings. Committee sets its own quorum and procedure.

nominated.note: Nominated by the VK Memorial Foundation (S.10)

composition.termLength: Not specified in Act — Board appointment

meetings.frequency: Not specified — Committee sets own procedure
meetings.quorum: Not specified — Committee sets own quorum (S.10)

powers: Day-to-day administration and management of the Hospital as delegated by the Board (S.10)
dataGaps: Exact composition (number of members) not specified in the Act
Current membership of the Committee not publicly documented
Whether the Committee meets regularly is unknown
Scope of delegated powers from the Board is unknown
Committee's own procedural rules not publicly accessible

timeline.details: Vijaya Kumaratunga, prominent actor, singer, and politician, was assassinated on 16 February 1988 at his home in Narahenpita. His wife Chandrika Bandaranaike Kumaratunga later became President of Sri Lanka.

Act No. 31 of 1998, effective 10 June 1998. Established the Vijaya Kumaratunga Memorial Foundation as a body corporate. Board of Management chaired by the President of the Republic, with the Leader of the Opposition, Chief Justice, and Auditor-General as ex-officio members. Foundation objectives: promote peace, youth education, cultural activities, and international understanding.

timeline.event: Vijaya Kumaratunga Memorial Hospital Board Act enacted
timeline.details: Act No. 38 of 1999, certified 11 November 1999. Establishes the VK Memorial Hospital Board as a body corporate with 7 members. 21 sections + Schedule. Creates a Committee of Management for day-to-day operations and a dedicated Fund for hospital finances. Hospital built on 3-acre site at Seeduwa on the Colombo-Negombo Highway.
The Vijaya Kumaratunga Memorial Hospital opened on 9 October 1999 (Vijaya Kumaratunga's birthday) with 60 beds, 26 staff, two 30-bed wards, OPD, and Emergency Treatment Unit.

timeline.event: Eye Ward and theatre complex added
timeline.details: Eye Ward and new operating theatre complex constructed. Hospital bed capacity increased to 148. Established the hospital as a regional centre for ophthalmology and cataract surgery.

On 8 November 2016, Cabinet approved a major expansion proposed by Minister (Dr.) Rajitha Senaratne: Rs. 35M for office/clinics building upgrade and Rs. 1,431M for a six-story building with Accident Ward, ETU, Medical/Surgical Wards, ICU, Eye Ward, Diabetic Centre, Laboratory, X-ray, and staff facilities.

The hospital celebrated its 25th anniversary in October 2024 with 137 beds. Services include medicine, surgery, ophthalmology (75,283+ cataract operations by 2014), OPD, ETU, endoscopy, and specialized clinics. Operates as a non-profit with nominal fees.

tiers.name: Minister
tiers.relationship: Policy authority: makes regulations (S.14), gives general directions on Board policy (S.11), approves bank accounts and borrowing (S.9(f),(g))

tiers.name: Vijaya Kumaratunga Memorial Hospital Board
tiers.relationship: Body corporate (S.2): 7 members appointed by Minister (including Foundation nominees); manages hospital, administers Fund, holds property, appoints staff; meets at least monthly with quorum of 4 (S.5); subject to Minister's directions (S.11)

tiers.name: Committee of Management
tiers.relationship: Subordinate body (S.10): chaired by Director of Hospital; handles day-to-day administration as delegated by Board; includes Foundation representative; Board Chairman may attend meetings

tiers.name: Hospital Staff
tiers.relationship: Public officers (S.16): Board may appoint staff (S.9(d)); public officers may be transferred/seconded with consent of relevant authority; staff deemed public servants under Penal Code (S.18)

currentReplacement.national: Minister of Health exercises policy authority over the Board; Board operates as an autonomous body corporate managing the hospital at Seeduwa
currentReplacement.provincial: Not applicable — hospital governance is a national function under this Act
currentReplacement.local: Not applicable — hospital serves the Seeduwa area and general public under the Board's management
